comment.body:
@GeorgeChernyshov codex-review will fail until you rebase the dev branch onto the base. 

https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/actions/runs/23776650470/job/69279953656

My local review though:
This is improved, but I don’t think it is fully resolved yet.

  The benchmark-specific SerialCommManager no longer falls back to periodic GET_STATE polling, so the unrelated polling-response path I called out earlier is largely removed. That addresses the biggest source of
  cross-iteration noise.

  However, the benchmark still advances a global currentIteration and swaps the latch before the previous iteration’s async response path is definitively finished. Completion is still driven by an uncorrelated
  WheelData callback / packet receipt path rather than by a response explicitly tied to the SET_MOTOR_LEVELS command for that iteration.

  So I’d consider the original concern partially addressed, but not fully resolved. The remaining gap is request/response attribution across iterations after timeout or delayed delivery. I’d treat this as
  resolved only once the benchmark associates completion marks with the specific command under test, rather than the current global-iteration async path.
